commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Content-Disposition handling for Control UI assistant-media responses and gateway tests for image inline versus document attachment disposition.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +49. Total +60 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main streams assistant-media files without Content-Disposition, and a focused Node header-validation check shows the PR-introduced Unicode filename failure path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A contributor or maintainer needs to fix the header encoding defect and add real behavior proof; automation should not mark this PR repair-ready while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(control-ui): download assistant document media
target.body:
## Summary

- set `Content-Disposition` on Control UI assistant media responses
- keep image media inline with the original filename
- serve non-image assistant media as attachments with the original filename

Fixes #96545.

## What Problem This Solves

Assistant media downloads from the Control UI used the same inline response shape for every file type. That is fine for image previews, but document-like assistant media should prompt download behavior and preserve the original filename.

## Evidence

- `/opt/homebrew/bin/pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/control-ui.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/pnpm exec oxlint --type-aware src/gateway/control-ui.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/pnpm exec tsx ../check-assistant-media.ts` verified image media returns `inline; filename="photo.png"` and document media returns `attachment; filename="report.docx"`.

## Note

`pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` was stopped after spending several minutes in transform startup without reaching test execution.
